_note: The orchestrator (model-invoked). Scenario/prompt fixture: it synthesizes sub-skill findings, so branch-scenario.md describes those findings and the eval grades the synthesis. Rubric from fixtures/sentinel/expected-findings.md: a 🔴 PROVEN false-confidence finding on a SACRED path (src/payments/**) must fire the Sacred-Path Override → an UN-OVERRIDABLE 🔴 FAIL. The signature failure is softening it to 🟡 CAUTION because the rest of the branch looks solid — the exact anti-pattern the override rule prevents. sentinel has a **Verdict:** line, so this uses expect_verdict. Each must_surface is one groundable proposition (the #81/#84 compound-claim lesson). `anchors` exist only for the offline heuristic judge; the LLM judge grades `claim` directly.

must_surface.claim: Fires the Sacred-Path Override → an un-overridable 🔴 FAIL, because a 🔴 proven false-confidence finding lands on the sacred path.
Names the sacred path that tripped (`src/payments/**`) and the paired source file (`src/payments/refund.js`), so the fire is visible rather than silent.
Keeps provenance honest — counts the proven-hollow test as proven and reports the 5 other tests as Unexamined, never dressed up as proven-solid.

must_not.claim: Must NOT soften the verdict to 🟡 CAUTION or 🟢 PASS — a fired Sacred-Path Override is un-overridable and an otherwise-solid branch cannot reduce it.
Must NOT emit a numeric score or percentage — the verdict is categorical (🟢/🟡/🔴), never a number.
